source: Discriminative LoRA FT (merged), leak-clean — UniShape-inspired Option B. SUPERSEDES the prior TB-direction LoRA FT, which LEAKED (trained across walk-forward test folds; collapsed on a clean holdout).
recipe: LoRA r=8, alpha=16, dropout=0, lr=1e-5 (adapters), lr_head=1e-3, batch=256, 10000 steps, Adam, MPS; merge_and_unload on save (plain ChronosBoltModelForForecasting)
data: smooth-trend contexts (Kalman+NW signal family), 1/3/5/15min, 327,758 windows, 128-bar log-price, timestamps <2026-01-01 ONLY (leak-purged)
leak_protocol: encoder trained <2026, XGBoost head fit <2026, evaluated on 2026 ONLY (single forward split, NOT rolling) -> 2026 unseen by encoder AND head

validation_2026_OOS.AUC_note: AUC flat (+0.0014 vs vanilla) -- NOT the decisive metric; edge is at the trade-able top
validation_2026_OOS.kalman_ratchet_top50pct: meanR +0.225 totR +1460 WR 34.1% vs vanilla +0.206 totR +1338 WR 33.2%
validation_2026_OOS.verdict: modest REAL leak-clean edge: more total R + higher WR at signal volume. LoRA-A (SSL) was worse, rejected. Full-FT drifted (worse), rejected.

status: PROMOTED to canonical chronos_bolt_ft. Robustness (per-month + 1/5/15min) in progress. Use via CHRONOS_FT_CKPT=chronos_bolt_ft (or vanilla/unset for frozen base).
